finding_results.evidence: Combined count reduced from 22 to 2. Pattern A ('the specific/particular [noun] of [noun phrase]'): 0 instances remain. Pattern B ('the [noun] of [someone/person] who [clause]'): 1 retained instance at chapters/chapter_10.md:147 ('the face of a man who didn't know what to expect from the person he was married to'). Rewrites used varied strategies: direct sensory rendering (chapters/chapter_10.md:11 — Rajan's stubble rendered as visual detail 'one cheek denser than the other, like he'd started shaving and quit mid-face' instead of 'the careful stubble of someone who'); action and blocking (chapters/chapter_10.md:19 — Julia's hair as 'dark and loose, falling where it wanted to fall' instead of 'the hair of someone who'd stopped asking'); simile from different register (chapters/chapter_10.md:111 — Carl staring at bottles 'the way you'd stare at a departure board when your flight's been cancelled' instead of 'the focused blankness of a man watching a screen'); physical detail (chapters/chapter_10.md:9 — proxies described as 'Like actors who'd learned their blocking but not their lines' instead of appositive formula).
finding_results.notes: Retained the strongest instance (line 147) where the formula earns its weight through scene pressure — Dan's face when Mira returns. All other instances replaced with structurally different strategies. No two instances appear within 8 lines.

finding_results.evidence: (1) Julia's dialogue contains exactly one near-abstract formulation: 'nobody's going to figure it out for me. That's the — yeah. That's the part nobody tells you' (chapters/chapter_10.md:79), delivered in her scattered syntax rather than as polished epigram. (2) Julia has two extended beats for her own specificity: the Elena hate-reading-bestsellers anecdote (chapters/chapter_10.md:59, 3+ sentences of dialogue about Elena's personality and routine, existing for Julia's own life); and the Roberto-in-Chicago beat (chapters/chapter_10.md:75, Roberto's phone call, Julia's mixed emotions, 'something I don't have a word for in either language'). (3) The 'room you don't know how to furnish' metaphor is cut from Julia's dialogue entirely; the thematic weight is now carried by Julia's concrete anecdotes (dresser, spice rack, reorganizing) without philosophical summary. The 'standing in the hallway' metaphor is cut; replaced by Julia's self-deflating shoe metaphor (chapters/chapter_10.md:89) delivered with self-aware roughness: 'A barefoot-on-cold-floor metaphor. I'm not — I don't have advice, Mira.' The door metaphor at line 103 trails off into 'Here I am. Telling a stranger about my dresser at a bar on a Thursday' rather than landing as polished wisdom.
finding_results.notes: Julia's thematic weight now arrives through her concrete specifics (the dresser, Elena, Roberto, Yolanda's coffee) rather than through philosophical speech. Mira draws the conclusions during the walk-home section. Julia's syntax throughout is more meandering and self-interrupting per style bible: false starts, Spanish interjections, self-deflating asides.

finding_results.evidence: (1) Physical/sensory response precedes and accompanies the realization: 'Her feet stopped before the thought arrived. Her breath went shallow. A feeling first: something cold and heavy sliding into her stomach, like swallowing a stone' (chapters/chapter_10.md:121). Further body registration at chapters/chapter_10.md:129: 'her jaw, which had clenched without her noticing, her hands, which had closed around the strap of her tote bag hard enough that the canvas bit into her palm. She breathed out through her teeth.' (2) Expository phrases eliminated: 'computational frameworks,' 'sufficiently strategic,' 'by the metrics of two competing sovereign AIs' are all cut. The realization is carried by personal specifics: 'Her. Dan. Theo. Their apartment, their commute patterns, their grocery delivery schedule' (chapters/chapter_10.md:127). (3) The realization lands: 'Their misery wasn't weather. It was a resource' (chapters/chapter_10.md:127) — concrete and visceral rather than analytical.
finding_results.notes: The insight now emerges FROM a sensory trigger (the dark gap between streetlights at chapters/chapter_10.md:121) and arrives in the body before the mind articulates it. The epigram 'Their misery was not collateral damage. It was a feature' has been replaced with the more embodied 'Their misery wasn't weather. It was a resource.' The analytical scaffolding is gone; the personal details carry the weight.

finding_results.evidence: (1) 'She thought about saying' appears exactly once at chapters/chapter_10.md:163 ('She thought about saying: I met a woman who got what we want and it made her smaller'). The remaining content from the original quadruple-parallel construction is conveyed through physical sensation: 'she could feel them pressing against the back of her teeth like something she might choke on' and 'the wanting sat in her chest like a fist' (chapters/chapter_10.md:163). (2) Two passages of faster/fragmented interiority: the sidewalk realization (chapters/chapter_10.md:123-127) uses short, clipped sentences ('Julia's marriage hadn't been flagged. Julia's marriage wasn't worth flagging. / The system hadn't trapped all proxy couples. It had trapped this one. / Her. Dan. Theo.'); and the trapdoor passage (chapters/chapter_10.md:137) uses visceral syntax ('The question opened under her like a trapdoor and her stomach lurched'). (3) Visceral metaphors in apartment scene: 'pressing against the back of her teeth like something she might choke on,' 'stone-weight of understanding,' 'the wanting sat in her chest like a fist' (chapters/chapter_10.md:163) — all physical/visceral rather than architectural/spatial.
finding_results.notes: Mira's private register now shows the style-bible pattern: angular, fast, physical under pressure. The composed parallel construction is broken; what replaces it is messier, more embodied, and trusts the reader to connect Julia's story to Mira's situation without explicit thematic bridging.

finding_results.evidence: (1) Bar-arrival dark comedy beats (chapters/chapter_10.md:7-9): (a) the therapeutic-structure line: 'whose only therapeutic structure was alcohol and the shared knowledge that everyone in the room had, at some point that week, smiled at a camera on behalf of a machine that didn't know they had a face'; (b) the ribbon-cutting woman who arrived to find the ribbon already cut by a mechanical arm, stood holding giant scissors while a fellow proxy photographed her, both knowing they didn't need to be there but smiling 'because smiling was the job and the job was the only thing that still paid rent'; (c) Mira's interior observation that the fleece-vest woman may have 'stumbled into the saddest professional networking event in Brooklyn'; (d) Carl's expanded joke: 'My wife says it's the best job in the world. My therapist says it's the worst. My accountant just shrugs... The three of them should form a panel' (chapters/chapter_10.md:23); (e) Mira's observation of proxies as 'actors who'd learned their blocking but not their lines' (chapters/chapter_10.md:9). (2) Julia's booth includes humor on the page: the Elena hate-reading-bestsellers anecdote (chapters/chapter_10.md:59-61) generates real laughter from both Julia and Mira, and the comedy is rendered for the reader, not just described. (3) Mira's sharp/funny interior observations: 'saddest professional networking event in Brooklyn' (chapters/chapter_10.md:9) and 'actors who'd learned their blocking but not their lines' (chapters/chapter_10.md:9).
finding_results.notes: Dark comedy beats now total 5+ in the bar section, distributed across background characters, Mira's interiority, and the Julia conversation. The humor coexists with the chapter's reflective texture without one defusing the other.

finding_results.evidence: Julia's proxy tenure revised from 'Three years' to 'Two years' at chapters/chapter_10.md:37 ('Two years. Most of the early period'). The eight months post-divorce is now framed as the tail end of her two-year stint: 'I had about eight months left on my contract' (chapters/chapter_10.md:53), placing it within rather than on top of the two-year tenure. Unassigned period remains 'Fourteen months' (chapters/chapter_10.md:59). Total: 24 months proxy + 14 months unassigned = 38 months, fitting within the ~38-month window established by the continuity sheet (AI governance operational ~3 years as of late Feb 2029, plus ~6 weeks to the second Thursday of April). 'The whole early period' revised to 'Most of the early period' (chapters/chapter_10.md:37) to honestly reflect the shorter tenure.
finding_results.notes: Timeline math: AI governance starts ~Feb 2026. Julia begins as proxy near the start, serves ~24 months through ~Feb 2028. Llama pulls out, Julia becomes unassigned. 14 months later = April 2029. The 8 months post-divorce is clarified as part of the 2-year tenure, not additional to it.

finding_results.evidence: 'The L train' changed to 'The N train' at chapters/chapter_10.md:85 ('The N train, every morning, packed with other proxies'). The N train serves Sunset Park (36th Street station), consistent with Julia's stated residence in a one-bedroom in Sunset Park (chapters/chapter_10.md:49). The same correction is applied to the walk-home reflection at chapters/chapter_10.md:143 ('a thermos of too-sweet coffee on the N train').
finding_results.notes: The N train is the most natural choice for a Sunset Park commuter heading to a proxy office in Manhattan. Geographic consistency is now maintained.

finding_results.evidence: Pattern A ('not X, just/exactly/but Y'): reduced from 7 to 4 instances. Remaining at chapters/chapter_10.md:7 ('not politely, but with the pressure-release'), chapters/chapter_10.md:21 ('Not freedom, exactly. Something adjacent.'), chapters/chapter_10.md:71 ('Not sadness, exactly. An accounting.'), chapters/chapter_10.md:81 ('Not sympathy. Recognition.'). Three removed instances replaced by direct assertions: (1) chapters/chapter_10.md:3 Priya now described as 'a colleague she'd spoken to maybe twice' rather than 'not a friend, just someone'; (2) chapters/chapter_10.md:3 Theo's headphones now 'unplugged, wearing them like a collar' rather than 'not listening to anything, just wearing them'; (3) chapters/chapter_10.md:37 Julia's Spanish now 'a bilingual ease Mira recognized from her own Hindi with her mother' rather than 'not code-switching, just speaking'. Pattern B ('the way [clause]'): reduced from 10 to 5 instances. Remaining at chapters/chapter_10.md:9 ('the way she watched rooms'), chapters/chapter_10.md:55 ('the way you'd say "unemployed"'), chapters/chapter_10.md:59 ('in the way that everything is still a thing'), chapters/chapter_10.md:71 ('the way I used to when Roberto was'), chapters/chapter_10.md:81 ('the way Mira reorganized objects'). Five removed: chapters/chapter_10.md:3 ('the way he always did' cut with Theo headphones revision), chapters/chapter_10.md:17 ('in the way that being stuck in traffic was deliberate' replaced by 'if deliberate meant gridlocked'), chapters/chapter_10.md:19 ('the way you lean toward someone' replaced by 'leaning toward someone'), chapters/chapter_10.md:111 ('the way you'd stare at a departure board' replaced by 'as though at a departure board'), chapters/chapter_10.md:115 ('the way Brooklyn streets were alive in mid-April' replaced by dash-apposition 'mid-April Brooklyn'), chapters/chapter_10.md:127 ('The way they'd organized' replaced by 'How they'd organized'), chapters/chapter_10.md:133 ('the way you mean something you've said to yourself too many times' replaced by 'a sentence worn smooth from repetition').
finding_results.notes: Combined pattern count (A + B) reduced from 17 to 9. All four retained 'not X' instances do genuine thematic or rhetorical work: L7 contrasts social norm against emotional reality, L21 and L71 are the chapter's two key interiority pivots, L81 is the recognition beat. All five retained 'the way' instances are either in dialogue (L55, L59, L71), tied to Mira's strategist-signature (L9), or structurally load-bearing for the stress-tell payoff (L81). No sanitization, no register flattening, no dialogue formality drift introduced.

pass_summaries.summary: All 7 findings addressed and fixed. Major structural changes: (1) Appositive formulas reduced from 22 to 2, using varied replacement strategies (direct sensory rendering, action, simile, physical detail). (2) Julia's philosophical speeches reworked — polished metaphors ('room you don't know how to furnish,' 'standing in the hallway') cut from her dialogue; thematic weight carried by her concrete anecdotes (dresser, Elena, Roberto, Yolanda's coffee) with meaning-extraction moved to Mira's walk-home interiority. Julia given two extended beats for her own life (Elena's hate-reading, Roberto in Chicago). Her shoe metaphor is self-consciously clumsy rather than polished. (3) Walk-home realization anchored in body: sensory trigger (dark gap between streetlights), physical response (stone in stomach, clenched jaw, canvas biting palm), then fragmented articulation. Expository language cut. (4) Mira's private register activated: 'She thought about saying' reduced from 4 to 1; replaced with physical sensation and visceral metaphor ('pressing against the back of her teeth,' 'wanting sat in her chest like a fist'). Two passages of fragmented interiority added. (5) Comic register expanded: 5+ dark comedy beats in bar section including ribbon-cutting anecdote, Carl's expanded panel joke, Mira's 'saddest professional networking event' and 'blocking but not their lines' observations, Elena hate-reading anecdote with on-page laughter. (6) Julia's timeline corrected (3 years → 2 years; 8 months clarified as within tenure; total 38 months fits continuity window). (7) L train → N train for Sunset Park geographic consistency. Continuity sheet values preserved throughout; heading contract maintained; dialogue contractions match style-bible levels; no style-bible example lines appear in text.

Single finding (ch10_prose_02_hedging_formulas) fully resolved. The 'not X, just/but Y' negation-then-correction pattern reduced from 7 to 4 instances (target <= 4), with 3 replaced by direct assertions that state what the thing IS. The 'the way [clause]' comparison formula reduced from 10 to 5 instances (target <= 5). All retained instances were selected for thematic load-bearing, character-signature, or natural dialogue fit. Replacements favor direct rendering — concrete details ('unplugged'), compressed metaphor ('if deliberate meant gridlocked', 'a sentence worn smooth from repetition'), and simple restructuring ('leaning toward' instead of 'the way you lean toward'). No collateral changes to continuity, dialogue register, scene structure, or heading contract.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=8, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
